endpoints.name: Authorize user
endpoints.description:
Authorize a user by sending them through the [Box](https://box.com)
website and request their permission to act on their behalf.

This is the first step when authenticating a user using
OAuth 2.0. To request a user's authorization to use the Box APIs
on their behalf you will need to send a user to the URL with this
format.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{BOX_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your BOX ACCESS TOKEN.

endpoints.name: List allowed collaboration domains
endpoints.description:
Returns the list domains that have been deemed safe to create collaborations
for within the current enterprise.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{BOX_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your BOX ACCESS TOKEN.

endpoints.name: Add domain to list of allowed collaboration domains
endpoints.description:
Creates a new entry in the list of allowed domains to allow
collaboration for.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{BOX_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your BOX ACCESS TOKEN.

endpoints.name: Get allowed collaboration domain
endpoints.description:
Returns a domain that has been deemed safe to create collaborations
for within the current enterprise.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{BOX_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your BOX ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{COLLABORATION_WHITELIST_ENTRY_ID}} with a real collaboration_whitelist_entry_id.

endpoints.name: Remove domain from list of allowed collaboration domains
endpoints.description:
Removes a domain from the list of domains that have been deemed safe to create
collaborations for within the current enterprise.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{BOX_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your BOX ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{COLLABORATION_WHITELIST_ENTRY_ID}} with a real collaboration_whitelist_entry_id.

endpoints.name: List users exempt from collaboration domain restrictions
endpoints.description:
Returns a list of users who have been exempt from the collaboration
domain restrictions.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{BOX_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your BOX ACCESS TOKEN.

endpoints.name: Create user exemption from collaboration domain restrictions
endpoints.description:
Exempts a user from the restrictions set out by the allowed list of domains
for collaborations.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{BOX_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your BOX ACCESS TOKEN.

endpoints.name: Get user exempt from collaboration domain restrictions
endpoints.description:
Returns a users who has been exempt from the collaboration
domain restrictions.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{BOX_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your BOX ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{COLLABORATION_WHITELIST_EXEMPT_TARGET_ID}} with a real collaboration_whitelist_exempt_target_id.

endpoints.name: Remove user from list of users exempt from domain restrictions
endpoints.description:
Removes a user's exemption from the restrictions set out by the allowed list
of domains for collaborations.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{BOX_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your BOX ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{COLLABORATION_WHITELIST_EXEMPT_TARGET_ID}} with a real collaboration_whitelist_exempt_target_id.

endpoints.name: List pending collaborations
endpoints.description: Retrieves all pending collaboration invites for this user.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{BOX_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your BOX ACCESS TOKEN.

endpoints.name: Create collaboration
endpoints.description:
Adds a collaboration for a single user or a single group to a file
or folder.

Collaborations can be created using email address, user IDs, or a
group IDs.

If a collaboration is being created with a group, access to
this endpoint is dependent on the group's ability to be invited.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{BOX_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your BOX ACCESS TOKEN.

endpoints.name: Get collaboration
endpoints.description: Retrieves a single collaboration.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{BOX_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your BOX ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{COLLABORATION_ID}} with a real collaboration_id.

endpoints.name: Update collaboration
endpoints.description:
Updates a collaboration.
Can be used to change the owner of an item, or to
accept collaboration invites.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{BOX_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your BOX ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{COLLABORATION_ID}} with a real collaboration_id.

endpoints.name: Remove collaboration
endpoints.description: Deletes a single collaboration.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{BOX_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your BOX ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{COLLABORATION_ID}} with a real collaboration_id.

endpoints.name: List all collections
endpoints.description:
Retrieves all collections for a given user.

Currently, only the `favorites` collection
is supported.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{BOX_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your BOX ACCESS TOKEN.

endpoints.name: List collection items
endpoints.description:
Retrieves the files and/or folders contained within
this collection.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{BOX_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your BOX ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{COLLECTION_ID}} with a real collection_id.

endpoints.name: Create comment
endpoints.description:
Adds a comment by the user to a specific file, or
as a reply to an other comment.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{BOX_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your BOX ACCESS TOKEN.

endpoints.name: Get comment
endpoints.description:
Retrieves the message and metadata for a specific comment, as well
as information on the user who created the comment.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{BOX_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your BOX ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{COMMENT_ID}} with a real comment_id.

endpoints.name: Update comment
endpoints.description: Update the message of a comment.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{BOX_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your BOX ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{COMMENT_ID}} with a real comment_id.

endpoints.name: Remove comment
endpoints.description: Permanently deletes a comment.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{BOX_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your BOX ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{COMMENT_ID}} with a real comment_id.

endpoints.name: Get device pin
endpoints.description: Retrieves information about an individual device pin.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{BOX_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your BOX ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{DEVICE_PINNER_ID}} with a real device_pinner_id.

endpoints.name: Remove device pin
endpoints.description: Deletes an individual device pin.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{BOX_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your BOX ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{DEVICE_PINNER_ID}} with a real device_pinner_id.

endpoints.name: List enterprise device pins
endpoints.description:
Retrieves all the device pins within an enterprise.

The user must have admin privileges, and the application
needs the "manage enterprise" scope to make this call.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{BOX_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your BOX ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{ENTERPRISE_ID}} with a real enterprise_id.

endpoints.name: List user and enterprise events
endpoints.description:
Returns up to a year of past events for a given user
or for the entire enterprise.

By default this returns events for the authenticated user. To retrieve events
for the entire enterprise, set the `stream_type` to `admin_logs_streaming`
for live monitoring of new events, or `admin_logs` for querying across
historical events. The user making the API call will
need to have admin privileges, and the application will need to have the
scope `manage enterprise properties` checked.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{BOX_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your BOX ACCESS TOKEN.

endpoints.name: Get events long poll endpoint
endpoints.description:
Returns a list of real-time servers that can be used for long-polling updates
to the [event stream](#get-events).

Long polling is the concept where a HTTP request is kept open until the
server sends a response, then repeating the process over and over to receive
updated responses.

Long polling the event stream can only be used for user events, not for
enterprise events.

To use long polling, first use this endpoint to retrieve a list of long poll
URLs. Next, make a long poll request to any of the provided URLs.

When an event occurs in monitored account a response with the value
`new_change` will be sent. The response contains no other details as
it only serves as a prompt to take further action such as sending a
request to the [events endpoint](#get-events) with the last known
`stream_position`.

After the server sends this response it closes the connection. You must now
repeat the long poll process to begin listening for events again.

If no events occur for a while and the connection times out you will
receive a response with the value `reconnect`. When you receive this response
you’ll make another call to this endpoint to restart the process.

If you receive no events in `retry_timeout` seconds then you will need to
make another request to the real-time server (one of the URLs in the response
for this endpoint). This might be necessary due to network errors.

Finally, if you receive a `max_retries` error when making a request to the
real-time server, you should start over by making a call to this endpoint
first.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{BOX_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your BOX ACCESS TOKEN.

endpoints.name: Get file request
endpoints.description: Retrieves the information about a file request.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{BOX_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your BOX ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{FILE_REQUEST_ID}} with a real file_request_id.

endpoints.name: Update file request
endpoints.description:
Updates a file request. This can be used to activate or
deactivate a file request.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{BOX_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your BOX ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{FILE_REQUEST_ID}} with a real file_request_id.

endpoints.name: Delete file request
endpoints.description: Deletes a file request permanently.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{BOX_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your BOX ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{FILE_REQUEST_ID}} with a real file_request_id.

endpoints.name: Copy file request
endpoints.description:
Copies an existing file request that is already present on one folder,
and applies it to another folder.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{BOX_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your BOX ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{FILE_REQUEST_ID}} with a real file_request_id.

endpoints.name: List file version legal holds
endpoints.description:
Get a list of file versions on legal hold for a legal hold
assignment.

Due to ongoing re-architecture efforts this API might not return all file
versions for this policy ID.

Instead, this API will only return file versions held in the legacy
architecture. Two new endpoints will available to request any file versions
held in the new architecture.

For file versions held in the new architecture, the `GET
/legal_hold_policy_assignments/:id/file_versions_on_hold` API can be used to
return all past file versions available for this policy assignment, and the
`GET /legal_hold_policy_assignments/:id/files_on_hold` API can be used to
return any current (latest) versions of a file under legal hold.

The `GET /legal_hold_policy_assignments?policy_id={id}` API can be used to
find a list of policy assignments for a given policy ID.

Once the re-architecture is completed this API will be deprecated.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{BOX_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your BOX ACCESS TOKEN.

endpoints.name: Get file version legal hold
endpoints.description:
Retrieves information about the legal hold policies
assigned to a file version.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{BOX_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your BOX ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{FILE_VERSION_LEGAL_HOLD_ID}} with a real file_version_legal_hold_id.

endpoints.name: List file version retentions
endpoints.description: Retrieves all file version retentions for the given enterprise.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{BOX_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your BOX ACCESS TOKEN.

endpoints.name: Get retention on file
endpoints.description: Returns information about a file version retention.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{BOX_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your BOX ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{FILE_VERSION_RETENTION_ID}} with a real file_version_retention_id.

endpoints.name: Upload file
endpoints.description:
Uploads a small file to Box. For file sizes over 50MB we recommend
using the Chunk Upload APIs.

# Request body order

The `attributes` part of the body must come **before** the
`file` part. Requests that do not follow this format when
uploading the file will receive a HTTP `400` error with a
`metadata_after_file_contents` error code.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{BOX_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your BOX ACCESS TOKEN.

endpoints.name: Preflight check before upload
endpoints.description:
Performs a check to verify that a file will be accepted by Box
before you upload the entire file.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{BOX_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your BOX ACCESS TOKEN.

endpoints.name: Create upload session
endpoints.description: Creates an upload session for a new file.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{BOX_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your BOX ACCESS TOKEN.

endpoints.name: Get upload session
endpoints.description: Return information about an upload session.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{BOX_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your BOX ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{UPLOAD_SESSION_ID}} with a real upload_session_id.

endpoints.name: Remove upload session
endpoints.description:
Abort an upload session and discard all data uploaded.

This cannot be reversed.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{BOX_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your BOX ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{UPLOAD_SESSION_ID}} with a real upload_session_id.

endpoints.name: Commit upload session
endpoints.description:
Close an upload session and create a file from the
uploaded chunks.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{BOX_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your BOX ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{UPLOAD_SESSION_ID}} with a real upload_session_id.

endpoints.name: List parts
endpoints.description:
Return a list of the chunks uploaded to the upload
session so far.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{BOX_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your BOX ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{UPLOAD_SESSION_ID}} with a real upload_session_id.

endpoints.name: Get file information
endpoints.description: Retrieves the details about a file.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{BOX_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your BOX ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{FILE_ID}} with a real file_id.

endpoints.name: Restore file
endpoints.description:
Restores a file that has been moved to the trash.

An optional new parent ID can be provided to restore the file to in case the
original folder has been deleted.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{BOX_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your BOX ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{FILE_ID}} with a real file_id.

endpoints.name: Update file
endpoints.description:
Updates a file. This can be used to rename or move a file,
create a shared link, or lock a file.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{BOX_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your BOX ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{FILE_ID}} with a real file_id.

endpoints.name: Delete file
endpoints.description:
Deletes a file, either permanently or by moving it to
the trash.

The the enterprise settings determine whether the item will
be permanently deleted from Box or moved to the trash.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{BOX_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your BOX ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{FILE_ID}} with a real file_id.

endpoints.name: Add shared link to file
endpoints.description: Adds a shared link to a file.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{BOX_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your BOX ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{FILE_ID}} with a real file_id.

endpoints.name: Get shared link for file
endpoints.description: Gets the information for a shared link on a file.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{BOX_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your BOX ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{FILE_ID}} with a real file_id.

endpoints.name: Remove shared link from file
endpoints.description: Removes a shared link from a file.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{BOX_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your BOX ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{FILE_ID}} with a real file_id.

endpoints.name: Update shared link on file
endpoints.description: Updates a shared link on a file.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{BOX_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your BOX ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{FILE_ID}} with a real file_id.

endpoints.name: List file collaborations
endpoints.description:
Retrieves a list of pending and active collaborations for a
file. This returns all the users that have access to the file
or have been invited to the file.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{BOX_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your BOX ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{FILE_ID}} with a real file_id.

endpoints.name: List file comments
endpoints.description: Retrieves a list of comments for a file.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{BOX_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your BOX ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{FILE_ID}} with a real file_id.

endpoints.name: Download file
endpoints.description: Returns the contents of a file in binary format.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{BOX_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your BOX ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{FILE_ID}} with a real file_id.

endpoints.name: Upload file version
endpoints.description:
Update a file's content. For file sizes over 50MB we recommend
using the Chunk Upload APIs.

# Request body order

The `attributes` part of the body must come **before** the
`file` part. Requests that do not follow this format when
uploading the file will receive a HTTP `400` error with a
`metadata_after_file_contents` error code.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{BOX_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your BOX ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{FILE_ID}} with a real file_id.

endpoints.name: Copy file
endpoints.description: Creates a copy of a file.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{BOX_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your BOX ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{FILE_ID}} with a real file_id.

endpoints.name: List metadata instances on file
endpoints.description: Retrieves all metadata for a given file.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{BOX_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your BOX ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{FILE_ID}} with a real file_id.

endpoints.name: Get classification on file
endpoints.description:
Retrieves the classification metadata instance that
has been applied to a file.

This API can also be called by including the enterprise ID in the
URL explicitly, for example
`/files/:id//enterprise_12345/securityClassification-6VMVochwUWo`.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{BOX_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your BOX ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{FILE_ID}} with a real file_id.

endpoints.name: Add classification to file
endpoints.description:
Adds a classification to a file by specifying the label of the
classification to add.

This API can also be called by including the enterprise ID in the
URL explicitly, for example
`/files/:id//enterprise_12345/securityClassification-6VMVochwUWo`.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{BOX_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your BOX ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{FILE_ID}} with a real file_id.

endpoints.name: Update classification on file
endpoints.description:
Updates a classification on a file.

The classification can only be updated if a classification has already been
applied to the file before. When editing classifications, only values are
defined for the enterprise will be accepted.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{BOX_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your BOX ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{FILE_ID}} with a real file_id.

endpoints.name: Remove classification from file
endpoints.description:
Removes any classifications from a file.

This API can also be called by including the enterprise ID in the
URL explicitly, for example
`/files/:id//enterprise_12345/securityClassification-6VMVochwUWo`.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{BOX_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your BOX ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{FILE_ID}} with a real file_id.

endpoints.name: List Box Skill cards on file
endpoints.description: List the Box Skills metadata cards that are attached to a file.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{BOX_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your BOX ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{FILE_ID}} with a real file_id.

endpoints.name: Create Box Skill cards on file
endpoints.description: Applies one or more Box Skills metadata cards to a file.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{BOX_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your BOX ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{FILE_ID}} with a real file_id.

endpoints.name: Update Box Skill cards on file
endpoints.description: Updates one or more Box Skills metadata cards to a file.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{BOX_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your BOX ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{FILE_ID}} with a real file_id.

endpoints.name: Remove Box Skill cards from file
endpoints.description: Removes any Box Skills cards metadata from a file.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{BOX_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your BOX ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{FILE_ID}} with a real file_id.

endpoints.name: Get metadata instance on file
endpoints.description:
Retrieves the instance of a metadata template that has been applied to a
file.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{BOX_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your BOX ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{FILE_ID}} with a real file_id. Replace {{SCOPE}} with a real scope. Replace {{TEMPLATE_KEY}} with a real template_key.

endpoints.name: Create metadata instance on file
endpoints.description:
Applies an instance of a metadata template to a file.

In most cases only values that are present in the metadata template
will be accepted, except for the `global.properties` template which accepts
any key-value pair.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{BOX_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your BOX ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{FILE_ID}} with a real file_id. Replace {{SCOPE}} with a real scope. Replace {{TEMPLATE_KEY}} with a real template_key.

endpoints.name: Update metadata instance on file
endpoints.description:
Updates a piece of metadata on a file.

The metadata instance can only be updated if the template has already been
applied to the file before. When editing metadata, only values that match
the metadata template schema will be accepted.

The update is applied atomically. If any errors occur during the
application of the operations, the metadata instance will not be changed.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{BOX_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your BOX ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{FILE_ID}} with a real file_id. Replace {{SCOPE}} with a real scope. Replace {{TEMPLATE_KEY}} with a real template_key.

endpoints.name: Remove metadata instance from file
endpoints.description: Deletes a piece of file metadata.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{BOX_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your BOX ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{FILE_ID}} with a real file_id. Replace {{SCOPE}} with a real scope. Replace {{TEMPLATE_KEY}} with a real template_key.

endpoints.name: List tasks on file
endpoints.description:
Retrieves a list of all the tasks for a file. This
endpoint does not support pagination.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{BOX_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your BOX ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{FILE_ID}} with a real file_id.

endpoints.name: Get file thumbnail
endpoints.description:
Retrieves a thumbnail, or smaller image representation, of a file.

Sizes of `32x32`,`64x64`, `128x128`, and `256x256` can be returned in
the `.png` format and sizes of `32x32`, `160x160`, and `320x320`
can be returned in the `.jpg` format.

Thumbnails can be generated for the image and video file formats listed
[found on our community site][1].

[1]: https://community.box.com/t5/Migrating-and-Previewing-Content/File-Types-and-Fonts-Supported-in-Box-Content-Preview/ta-p/327
endpoints.notes: Replace {{BOX_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your BOX ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{FILE_ID}} with a real file_id. Replace {{EXTENSION}} with a real extension.

endpoints.name: Get trashed file
endpoints.description:
Retrieves a file that has been moved to the trash.

Please note that only if the file itself has been moved to the
trash can it be retrieved with this API call. If instead one of
its parent folders was moved to the trash, only that folder
can be inspected using the
[`GET /folders/:id/trash`](e://get_folders_id_trash) API.

To list all items that have been moved to the trash, please
use the [`GET /folders/trash/items`](e://get-folders-trash-items/)
API.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{BOX_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your BOX ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{FILE_ID}} with a real file_id.

endpoints.name: Permanently remove file
endpoints.description:
Permanently deletes a file that is in the trash.
This action cannot be undone.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{BOX_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your BOX ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{FILE_ID}} with a real file_id.

endpoints.name: Create upload session for existing file
endpoints.description: Creates an upload session for an existing file.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{BOX_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your BOX ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{FILE_ID}} with a real file_id.

endpoints.name: List all file versions
endpoints.description:
Retrieve a list of the past versions for a file.

Versions are only tracked by Box users with premium accounts. To fetch the ID
of the current version of a file, use the `GET /file/:id` API.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{BOX_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your BOX ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{FILE_ID}} with a real file_id.

endpoints.name: Promote file version
endpoints.description:
Promote a specific version of a file.

If previous versions exist, this method can be used to
promote one of the older versions to the top of the version history.

This creates a new copy of the old version and puts it at the
top of the versions history. The file will have the exact same contents
as the older version, with the the same hash digest, `etag`, and
name as the original.

Other properties such as comments do not get updated to their
former values.

Don't use this endpoint to restore Box Notes,
as it works with file formats such as PDF, DOC,
PPTX or similar.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{BOX_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your BOX ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{FILE_ID}} with a real file_id.

endpoints.name: Get file version
endpoints.description:
Retrieve a specific version of a file.

Versions are only tracked for Box users with premium accounts.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{BOX_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your BOX ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{FILE_ID}} with a real file_id. Replace {{FILE_VERSION_ID}} with a real file_version_id.

endpoints.name: Restore file version
endpoints.description:
Restores a specific version of a file after it was deleted.
Don't use this endpoint to restore Box Notes,
as it works with file formats such as PDF, DOC,
PPTX or similar.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{BOX_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your BOX ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{FILE_ID}} with a real file_id. Replace {{FILE_VERSION_ID}} with a real file_version_id.

endpoints.name: Remove file version
endpoints.description:
Move a file version to the trash.

Versions are only tracked for Box users with premium accounts.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{BOX_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your BOX ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{FILE_ID}} with a real file_id. Replace {{FILE_VERSION_ID}} with a real file_version_id.

endpoints.name: Get watermark on file
endpoints.description: Retrieve the watermark for a file.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{BOX_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your BOX ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{FILE_ID}} with a real file_id.

endpoints.name: Apply watermark to file
endpoints.description: Applies or update a watermark on a file.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{BOX_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your BOX ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{FILE_ID}} with a real file_id.

endpoints.name: Remove watermark from file
endpoints.description: Removes the watermark from a file.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{BOX_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your BOX ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{FILE_ID}} with a real file_id.

endpoints.name: List folder locks
endpoints.description:
Retrieves folder lock details for a given folder.

You must be authenticated as the owner or co-owner of the folder to
use this endpoint.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{BOX_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your BOX ACCESS TOKEN.

endpoints.name: Create folder lock
endpoints.description:
Creates a folder lock on a folder, preventing it from being moved and/or
deleted.

You must be authenticated as the owner or co-owner of the folder to
use this endpoint.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{BOX_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your BOX ACCESS TOKEN.

endpoints.name: Delete folder lock
endpoints.description:
Deletes a folder lock on a given folder.

You must be authenticated as the owner or co-owner of the folder to
use this endpoint.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{BOX_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your BOX ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{FOLDER_LOCK_ID}} with a real folder_lock_id.

endpoints.name: Create folder
endpoints.description: Creates a new empty folder within the specified parent folder.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{BOX_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your BOX ACCESS TOKEN.

endpoints.name: List trashed items
endpoints.description:
Retrieves the files and folders that have been moved
to the trash.

Any attribute in the full files or folders objects can be passed
in with the `fields` parameter to retrieve those specific
attributes that are not returned by default.

This endpoint defaults to use offset-based pagination, yet also supports
marker-based pagination using the `marker` parameter.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{BOX_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your BOX ACCESS TOKEN.

endpoints.name: Get folder information
endpoints.description:
Retrieves details for a folder, including the first 100 entries
in the folder.

To fetch more items within the folder, please use the
[Get items in a folder](#get-folders-id-items) endpoint.

endpoints.name: Restore folder

An optional new parent ID can be provided to restore the folder to in case the
original folder has been deleted.

# Folder locking

During this operation, part of the file tree will be locked, mainly
the source folder and all of its descendants, as well as the destination
folder.

For the duration of the operation, no other move, copy, delete, or restore
operation can performed on any of the locked folders.

instead to return any current (latest) versions of a file for this legal hold
policy assignment.

Due to ongoing re-architecture efforts this API might not return all files
held for this policy ID. Instead, this API will only return past file versions
held in the newly developed architecture. The `GET /file_version_legal_holds`
API can be used to fetch current and past versions of files held within the
legacy architecture.

The `GET /legal_hold_policy_assignments?policy_id={id}` API can be used to
find a list of policy assignments for a given policy ID.

API instead to return any previous versions of a file for this legal hold
policy assignment.

versions held for this policy ID. Instead, this API will only return the
latest file version held in the newly developed architecture. The `GET
/file_version_legal_holds` API can be used to fetch current and past versions

endpoints.name: Transfer owned folders
endpoints.description:
Move all of the items (files, folders and workflows) owned by a user into
another user's account

Only the root folder (`0`) can be transferred.

Folders can only be moved across users by users with administrative
permissions.

All existing shared links and folder-level collaborations are transferred
during the operation. Please note that while collaborations at the individual
file-level are transferred during the operation, the collaborations are
deleted when the original user is deleted.

This call will be performed synchronously which might lead to a slow response
when the source user has a large number of items in all of its folders.

If the destination path has a metadata cascade policy attached to any of
the parent folders, a metadata cascade operation will be kicked off
asynchronously.

There is currently no way to check for when this operation is finished.

The destination folder's name will be in the format `{User}'s Files and
Folders`, where `{User}` is the display name of the user.

To make this API call your application will need to have the "Read and write
all files and folders stored in Box" scope enabled.

Please make sure the destination user has access to `Relay` or `Relay Lite`,
and has access to the files and folders involved in the workflows being
transferred.

Admins will receive an email when the operation is completed.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{BOX_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your BOX ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{USER_ID}} with a real user_id.

endpoints.name: Create zip download
endpoints.description:
Creates a request to download multiple files and folders as a single `zip`
archive file. This API does not return the archive but instead performs all
the checks to ensure that the user has access to all the items, and then
returns a `download_url` and a `status_url` that can be used to download the
archive.

The limit for an archive is either the Account's upload limit or
10,000 files, whichever is met first
endpoints.notes: Replace {{BOX_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your BOX ACCESS TOKEN.

endpoints.name: Download zip archive
endpoints.description:
Returns the contents of a `zip` archive in binary format. This URL does not
require any form of authentication and could be used in a user's browser to
download the archive to a user's device.

By default, this URL is only valid for a few seconds from the creation of
the request for this archive. Once a download has started it can not be
stopped and resumed, instead a new request for a zip archive would need to
be created.

The URL of this endpoint should not be considered as fixed. Instead, use
the [Create zip download](e://post_zip_downloads) API to request to create a
`zip` archive, and then follow the `download_url` field in the response to
this endpoint.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{BOX_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your BOX ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{ZIP_DOWNLOAD_ID}} with a real zip_download_id.

endpoints.name: Get zip download status
endpoints.description:
Returns the download status of a `zip` archive, allowing an application to
inspect the progress of the download as well as the number of items that
might have been skipped.

This endpoint can only be accessed once the download has started.
Subsequently this endpoint is valid for 12 hours from the start of the
download.

The URL of this endpoint should not be considered as fixed. Instead, use
the [Create zip download](e://post_zip_downloads) API to request to create a
`zip` archive, and then follow the `status_url` field in the response to
this endpoint.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{BOX_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your BOX ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{ZIP_DOWNLOAD_ID}} with a real zip_download_id.
